counter_argument: Stage-1 casual game with no accounts, no money, ephemeral identity (CONFIRM-01 trust-the-client); real-world impact is griefing/turn-hijacking within a single room an attacker already knows the id of, not data or financial loss. But the model explicitly sanctions only self-cheating, NOT hijacking another player's seat, and security-controls.md names this exact control as enforced when it is not — so the gap between documented posture and reality is real.
rationale: Verified end-to-end: anon USING(true) SELECT on rooms exposes players JSONB (with id) + REPLICA IDENTITY FULL Realtime; authorizeAction gates on that same id; playerId=actingId=victimId defeats all three regimes. Clear engineering fix (stop exposing the token via public read).

Confirmed: typeof-number check admits NaN/Infinity/negative maxWind/gravity into the deterministic engine; sibling economy opts ARE clamped, so this is an inconsistency. Bounded to opt-in room participants; low. Client rematch read repeats the same weak fallback.

Real god module (2217 LOC fusing view+transport+Realtime+validation), but pure maintainability/testability debt with no correctness/security defect; downgraded high->medium for Stage-1 lighter gates. The trust-boundary-in-UI angle is a mild risk amplifier, kept as medium not high.
HUD.build() 590-LOC monolith is a subset of the broader HUD.ts decomposition; merge with architecture-003 into one HUD-split issue.
HUD.ts 2120-LOC god module; same remediation family as architecture-002 (extract modals/widgets). One combined issue.

10 hand-rolled functions/v1 fetch sites, no shared transport; real cross-cutting duplication, low severity. Related to but distinct from architecture-001 (this is the repo-wide transport helper; 001 is the Lobby split).

GameEngine.tick() ~230 LOC in the hard-determinism core; behavior-preserving decomposition would let harnesses assert per-phase tick determinism in isolation. Real maintainability finding in the highest-consequence file; medium.

Coherent, code-grounded race (fetch-then-subscribe gap + first-SUBSCRIBED skips resync via _everSubscribed guard -> seq-hole wedge), but observed=false and 'permanent stall' has genuine uncertainty (reconnect/resyncLog recovery path). Below the 0.7 medium gate -> investigate, preserved for follow-up not filed.

Well-reasoned single-driver bot-stall (lastBotKey latched pre-POST, fire-and-forget no retry, bot turns excluded from watchdog), but observed=false, narrow config (networked + exactly one human + CPU + transient failure). Below 0.7 medium gate -> investigate.

Confirmed lifecycle gap: stop() clears rAF/watchdog/channels but not the seq-conflict retry setTimeout nor the handleRematch poll loop, so post-teardown callbacks can fire. Low blast radius (referee rejects late POST) but a concrete actionable fix (dispose flag).

counter_argument: The DELETE-cascade (data-loss) path requires a room's roster to look fully-stale in the list_rooms snapshot AND the room to flip waiting->active in the sub-second gap before the reap RPC — a narrow timing intersection with no attacker leverage, ordinarily rare. The more reachable UPDATE-trim path reverts one ready and is user-recoverable by re-readying. Hence high, not critical.
rationale: Verified: 007 apply_room_reap DELETE and UPDATE both lack a status='waiting' predicate; ready_up flips status to active with no write-time status re-check; list_rooms reaps from an earlier snapshot. Real correctness/data-integrity race; DELETE cascades to room_actions (loses the canonical action log). Trivial fix (AND status='waiting' + .eq on ready_up write), effort S. Highest-priority fix behind the critical.
Missing data-classification comment on rooms/room_actions/match_scores vs the convention 005 set. Real but a doc-comment nicety; confidence 0.7 below the 0.75 low gate -> investigate.

Confirmed: 008 CREATE INDEX on write-hot rate_limits without CONCURRENTLY. Sub-second today, but establishes a locking-DDL pattern on a per-request table. Actionable: use CONCURRENTLY (outside the migration txn) or document the accepted trade-off. Low.

Confirmed: ~9 of ~13 Edge Function console.error sites log the full Postgrest error object (schema/constraint details) vs the .message pattern already adopted in 2 sites. No secret exposed; aids schema enumeration in dashboard logs. Concrete fix, effort S. Low.

withCors preamble (all 10 fns: OPTIONS/405/400/429/fail-open) untested. Downgraded high->medium: a coverage gap is absence-of-test, not a live defect. High-consequence shared gate; effort S. Combine with coverage-002 (same file mod.test.ts, same remediation).
reap() staleness filter (seat reuse / occupancy) untested at the inclusive boundary + missing-lastSeen default; drives room.players writes. Effort S; same mod.test.ts backfill as coverage-001.

NetworkClient self-heal orchestration not instantiated by any test. Mitigated: lockstep.mjs tests a parallel re-implementation of the exact sequencing discipline, so the ALGORITHM is covered, only the class wiring isn't. Downgraded high->medium (coverage gap); requires a DI/transport-injection refactor (effort M). Below the 0.7 medium gate -> investigate.

submit_action live handler body (isMember gate, actingId defaulting, validatedAction assembly) behind import.meta.main, untested; pure helpers ARE tested. Real gap on the risk-#1 function but needs extraction/DI to test; confidence 0.6 below the 0.7 medium gate -> investigate.

Verified: all 3 workflows use floating @vN tags, no SHA pins; deploy-pages injects VITE_SUPABASE_* secrets + has pages:write/id-token:write. Tag-move/upstream-compromise supply-chain risk; heightened as the repo goes public (fork-triggered CI). Effort S. Medium.

Verified by full-file read: ci.yml has no permissions: block (contrast deploy-pages/codeql which scope down). Least-privilege gap, more relevant as repo goes public + accepts fork PRs. Trivial fix (permissions: contents: read). Low.

DB-error console.error omits in-scope roomId/playerId on 9/10 functions, inconsistent with the #67/#79 context convention. Certain fact but marginal Stage-1 diagnosability polish; 0.65 below the 0.7 medium gate -> investigate.

6 Lobby.ts network catches discard the caught err with zero console signal (no APM in stack). Real (browser console is the only diagnostic surface) but Stage-1 polish; 0.65 below the 0.7 medium gate -> investigate. Pairs with observability-001 as a 'apply logging convention consistently' follow-up.

ServiceClient=any erases tsc checking on the read path across all 10 Edge Functions (the untrusted boundary, tsc --noEmit is the sole static gate, no CI deno test). Casts assert unchecked shapes; a column typo/schema drift compiles clean, fails only in prod. Systemic footgun; fix = gen types or a per-table parse helper. Medium.
